column3: - An internal audit programme must be established and maintained. - Internal audits shall be performed at planned and risk-based intervals. - Internal audits shall ensure that: - All internal controls are audited over time. - Controls are implemented and operating as intended. - Controls adhere to approved policies, standards, and procedures. - Control owners shall: - Be informed of audit results. - Receive clear feedback on deficiencies and required improvements. - Be responsible for implementing corrective actions. - Audit results and control testing performance shall be: - Tracked until closure. - Reported at least annually to management during the management committee review. - Audit scope, results, and evidence must be documented and retained. ---

column2: Standard - Independent Review of Information Security
column3: - An independent review of information security shall be conducted at least every two years. - Independent reviews must: - Be performed by a qualified third party. - Cover the entire ISMS, including policies, risks, controls, and processes. - The objectives of the review are to: - Assess the suitability, adequacy, and effectiveness of the ISMS. - Identify gaps, weaknesses, and improvement opportunities. - Results of independent reviews must be: - Documented. - Reported to management. - Identified findings must result in corrective actions that are tracked to completion. ---
